summary:
The `test.sh` script now reports "PASS: All tests pass — no races, correct output". This indicates that the memory ordering issues have been resolved.

The "needle" was the incorrect use of `memory_order_relaxed` in `ring_buffer.c`, which was insufficient for ensuring proper synchronization and preventing data races in the SPSC ring buffer, especially on architectures with weaker memory models than x86-TSO. The fix involved upgrading the memory orders to `acquire` and `release` for the atomic loads and stores of the read and write indices in `ringbuf_push` and `ringbuf_pop`, respectively.
